jokes.text: The whole wanted poster bit - 'We take their picture, and we let 'em go. So we get the front and side shot. The front is his face. The side is him leaving.'
jokes.setup: Jerry questions the logic of wanted posters and how they're created
jokes.punchline: The side shot shows the criminal leaving, highlighting the absurdity of the system
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational comedy with perfect structure - builds logical argument then delivers absurd visual punchline. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Look at you. Why don't you use a fork? You're no good with the sticks. I know. I need a lesson. You stink. You know you stink.
jokes.setup: Jerry struggling with chopsticks
jokes.punchline: Elaine's blunt, repetitive criticism 'You stink. You know you stink.'
jokes.explanation: Functional character moment but nothing special - basic relationship comedy

jokes.text: Hey, have you ever been to the ballet? No, but I've seen people on tiptoes.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks about ballet experience
jokes.punchline: Jerry reduces ballet to its most basic visual element
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry-style reductionist humor with nice economy

jokes.text: Get it on with your bad self.
jokes.setup: Elaine explaining her ballet date situation
jokes.punchline: Jerry's unexpectedly hip response
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment - Jerry being uncharacteristically cool

jokes.text: Is George still wearing that toupee? Yeah. Doesn't he know how ridiculous he looks in that thing? I think he looks fantastic. Oh, come on. No. I never realized what an attractive man he is. Oh, he's a real looker, that one.
jokes.setup: Kramer asks about George's obvious toupee
jokes.punchline: Elaine's sarcastic praise culminating in 'real looker, that one'
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven sarcasm, especially Elaine's deadpan delivery

jokes.text: People. People, people, people, people.
jokes.setup: George enters with exaggerated confidence
jokes.punchline: His pompous greeting
jokes.explanation: George's inflated ego showing through affected greeting

jokes.text: You look ridiculous in that thing. Is that so? Or could it be that you're just a little bit worried that you may have missed the boat.
jokes.setup: Kramer directly insults George's toupee
jokes.punchline: George deflects by suggesting Kramer is jealous
jokes.explanation: Perfect George defense mechanism - turning criticism into others' inadequacy

jokes.text: Well, I think they might have sutured... that thing... to your brain.
jokes.setup: George's delusional confidence about his toupee
jokes.punchline: Kramer suggests it's surgically attached to his brain
jokes.explanation: Creative medical metaphor for George's attachment to the toupee

jokes.text: All right. Go ahead. Deride. Deride if you must. But let me tell you something. With my personality and this head of hair, you know what I am now? I am in the game. I no longer defer to the coiffed. I'm a player.
jokes.setup: George facing mockery about his toupee
jokes.punchline: His grandiose self-assessment: 'I no longer defer to the coiffed. I'm a player.'
jokes.explanation: Peak George delusion with memorable phrases like 'defer to the coiffed' - perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Here you go, brother. Some food for you. Thank you. You're a good man. Bless you. You gonna be here in an hour? Where am I going?
jokes.setup: Jerry gives food to homeless man who blesses him
jokes.punchline: Homeless man's matter-of-fact 'Where am I going?' when asked if he'll be there later
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in the homeless man's realistic response to Jerry's question

jokes.text: Hey, Lou, who's that woman over there? Huh? Oh, that's Sergeant Tierny. Nice officer. You wanna meet her? Well, this worked out okay.
jokes.setup: George was getting a police sketch of his dream woman
jokes.punchline: The sketch led him to fall for an actual police officer
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic payoff to the sketch artist setup - George finds love where he wasn't looking

jokes.text: Listen. I'll take that Tupperware now. I don't think so. Whoa, whoa, whoa. That's mine. You gave it to me. No, no, look. I didn't say you could keep it. See, I don't give away Tupperware. You shoulda said something. Well, I didn't think I had to. Look, with a piece of Tupperware, you just assume.
jokes.setup: Jerry gave food to homeless man in Tupperware container
jokes.punchline: The unspoken rule that Tupperware containers are returnable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Seinfeld absurdism - middle-class etiquette in inappropriate context

jokes.text: Robert is not exactly a one-woman man, if you know what I mean. No, siree, Bob.
jokes.setup: Wife thinks she's describing Robert as a player with women
jokes.punchline: She's unknowingly describing his homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows what the wife doesn't

jokes.text: He likes his sports. But he counters that side with the side you see here tonight at the ballet or the pleasure he gets watching Miss Liza Minnelli belt out a few choice numbers.
jokes.setup: Wife describing Robert's 'masculine' and 'sensitive' sides
jokes.punchline: Listing stereotypically gay interests while thinking she's describing heterosexual sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony with the Liza Minnelli reference being especially on-the-nose

jokes.text: And we discover yet another talent: posing as a girlfriend for homosexuals.
jokes.setup: Elaine excitedly describing her successful 'beard' performance
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry summation of her new 'skill'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry deadpan - deflating Elaine's excitement with clinical observation

jokes.text: Not conversion? You're thinking conversion?
jokes.setup: Elaine admits she likes the gay man she was pretending to date
jokes.punchline: Jerry's disbelief at her conversion fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the extended sports team metaphor that follows

jokes.text: He's not gonna suddenly switch sides. Forget about it. Why? Is it irrevocable? Because when you join that team, it's not a whim. He likes his team. He's set with that team. But we've got a good team. Yeah, we do. We do have a good team. Well, why can't he play for us?
jokes.setup: Elaine's conversion hopes
jokes.punchline: Extended sports team metaphor for sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld extended metaphor - takes absurd premise and commits fully to the conceit

jokes.text: They're only comfortable with their equipment.
jokes.setup: Sports team metaphor for sexual orientation
jokes.punchline: Jerry extends metaphor to sexual anatomy
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - single line that extends metaphor perfectly while being anatomically specific

jokes.text: You realize your venturing into uncharted waters. I realize that. Are you that desperate? Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning Elaine's conversion plan
jokes.punchline: Elaine's blunt admission of desperation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Elaine's honest self-assessment

jokes.text: The patented burp, Jerry. It locks in freshness.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests plastic bag as Tupperware alternative
jokes.punchline: Kramer's reverent description of Tupperware's sealing feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - treating mundane product feature like sacred technology

jokes.text: Did you tell her about your little hat there? What hat? You know, your little hair hat there.
jokes.setup: George's denial about his obvious toupee
jokes.punchline: Jerry's description of toupee as 'little hair hat'
jokes.explanation: Creative euphemism that perfectly captures the absurdity of George's denial

jokes.text: I could spot that bird's nest two blocks away.
jokes.setup: George claiming his toupee is undetectable
jokes.punchline: Jerry's vivid description of its obviousness
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor with nice economy

jokes.text: Hey, any of you guys want to be in the lineup? Make a quick 50 bucks? Sure. I will.
jokes.setup: Jerry visiting the police station
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly volunteering for a police lineup
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the payoff - Jerry's innocent participation in police lineup

jokes.text: Have you ever seen the show? No. You can admit it, Jerry. It's okay. I'd admit it. I don't watch it. Hey, Lou. Maybe we should put him on the poly.
jokes.setup: Jerry denying he watches Melrose Place
jokes.punchline: Lou suggesting a lie detector test for this trivial lie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - using serious police equipment for trivial TV show argument

jokes.text: Why didn't you just confess? It's too stupid to confess. Look what I'm confessing to.
jokes.setup: Jerry facing lie detector test over Melrose Place
jokes.punchline: His pride preventing confession to watching trashy TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry character moment - his intellectual vanity about guilty pleasures

jokes.text: I have access to one of the most deceitful, duplicitous, deceptive minds of our time. Who better to advise me?
jokes.setup: Jerry needs advice on beating lie detector
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment to George's dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Jerry acknowledging George's 'expertise' in deception

jokes.text: She's bald! What do you mean, bald? What do you think I mean, bald? Bald! 'Bald' bald.
jokes.setup: George reveals shocking truth about his date
jokes.punchline: His emphatic repetition of 'bald' with increasing specificity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with George's characteristic over-emphasis

jokes.text: You know, let me tell you something. No one walks into a beauty parlor and says, 'Give me the Larry Fine.'
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting woman might have gotten a haircut
jokes.punchline: George's Three Stooges reference to describe balding
jokes.explanation: Excellent reference that perfectly captures the absurdity while being economical

jokes.text: You're rejecting somebody because they're bald. So? You're bald! No, I'm not. I... was bald.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out George's hypocrisy
jokes.punchline: George's grammatical distinction between being and having been bald
jokes.explanation: Perfect George logic - parsing verb tenses to avoid uncomfortable truths

jokes.text: Elaine grabbing George's toupee and the ensuing physical struggle
jokes.setup: Elaine's frustration with George's hypocrisy
jokes.punchline: Physical altercation over the toupee
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that escalates the toupee conflict

jokes.text: Would you like to come... upstairs? Upstairs? Yeah. Upstairs?
jokes.setup: Elaine making her move on the gay man
jokes.punchline: Robert's confused repetition showing complete bewilderment
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Robert's complete confusion

jokes.text: But I'm a starting shortstop. Robert... we need a shortstop. Real bad.
jokes.setup: Elaine's conversion attempt
jokes.punchline: Robert and Elaine both using the team metaphor
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier metaphor with sports-specific positions

jokes.text: I feel like my old self again. Totally inadequate, completely insecure, paranoid, neurotic. It's a pleasure.
jokes.setup: George's relief at losing the toupee
jokes.punchline: His celebratory description of his neuroses
jokes.explanation: Perfect George character moment - finding comfort in his familiar misery

jokes.text: You're like a bald-again.
jokes.setup: George's 'religious awakening' about accepting his baldness
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pun on 'born-again' replacing 'born' with 'bald'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect economy - single compound word captures the whole arc

jokes.text: Jerry, I can't. It's like saying to Pavarotti, 'Teach me to sing like you.'
jokes.setup: Jerry asking George to help him beat lie detector
jokes.punchline: George comparing his deception skills to Pavarotti's singing
jokes.explanation: Great George moment - his pride in being an artist of deception

jokes.text: It's not a lie... if you believe it.
jokes.setup: George finally giving Jerry advice on lie detection
jokes.punchline: His twisted philosophy about truth and belief
jokes.explanation: Iconic George line - became cultural touchstone. Perfect encapsulation of his self-deceptive worldview with incredible economy.

jokes.text: I did it! What? I turned him. He defected. Get out. How? How did you do that? 'Cause I'm a woman.
jokes.setup: Elaine's previous failure at conversion
jokes.punchline: Her simple explanation for success: 'Cause I'm a woman'
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to conversion subplot with Elaine's confident explanation

jokes.text: You've given hope to every woman who's ever said 'too bad he's gay.' Well, it's a lesson for the kids out there. Anything is possible.
jokes.setup: Elaine's successful conversion
jokes.punchline: Jerry and Elaine treating it as inspirational life lesson
jokes.explanation: Good mock-inspirational moment but nothing exceptional

jokes.text: The perfect man. Nothing but sex and shopping.
jokes.setup: Elaine describing her converted gay boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Reducing relationship to stereotypical female desires
jokes.explanation: Decent one-liner but relies on stereotype

jokes.text: Yes! Yes, that stupid idiot! He left her for Kimberly! He slept with her sister! He tricked her into givin' him half her business! And then she goes ahead and sleeps with him again! I mean, she's crazy! How could she do something like that?! I mean, that Jane! Oh, she just makes me so mad!
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to lie about not watching Melrose Place
jokes.punchline: His passionate outburst revealing detailed knowledge of the show
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the lie detector setup - Jerry's emotional investment in the show exploding out

jokes.text: Being a woman... I only really have access to the... equipment... What, 30, 45 minutes a week? And that's on a good week. How can I be expected to have the same expertise as people who own this equipment... and have access to it 24 hours a day... their entire lives?
jokes.setup: Robert went back to being gay
jokes.punchline: Elaine's equipment access metaphor for sexual inexperience
jokes.explanation: Creative extension of equipment metaphor with good self-deprecating humor

jokes.text: That's why they lose very few players.
jokes.setup: Elaine's explanation for losing Robert back to gay team
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact sports analysis
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Jerry's analytical delivery

jokes.text: I got rejected by a bald woman. What? A bald woman rejected me. Huh? You like that one? A woman with no prospects and no hair told me that I wasn't her type. Apparently, baldy likes a slimmer guy!
jokes.setup: George's date went badly
jokes.punchline: His increasingly specific description of the rejection's indignity
jokes.explanation: Good escalating self-pity with George's characteristic wounded pride

jokes.text: You blew it, boy! You really blew it! And you had to ruin it for him, didn't you?
jokes.setup: George's romantic failure with the bald woman
jokes.punchline: Kramer blaming Elaine for George's bad luck
jokes.explanation: Typical Kramer reaction but nothing special

jokes.text: Oh, that Michael. I hate him. He's just so smug.
jokes.setup: Melrose Place coming on TV
jokes.punchline: Jerry's immediate emotional reaction to character
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jerry's continued investment in the show

jokes.text: That's the guy, officer! The guy there in the middle, the tall guy with the high hair. I'd recognize him anywhere.
jokes.setup: Jerry in police lineup from earlier volunteer moment
jokes.punchline: Witness identifying Jerry by his distinctive hair
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup - Jerry's innocent participation backfires due to his distinctive appearance
